beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check rooftop herb garden in the drizzle.
beats.reasoning: Morning routine anchored at home; low energy yesterday (40/100) means a slower, quieter start. Mei checks her garden habit even in grey weather—it's a ritual.

beats.activity: Commute to Stoup Brewing, taking I-90 westbound to avoid SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon forces a longer route via I-90; budgeted 50 min to account for rain and detour. Arriving before the worst traffic.

beats.activity: Brewing shift: prepping fermentation tanks, monitoring pH, quality control.
beats.reasoning: Mei is a brewer; morning shift aligns with typical brewery schedule. Rain outside means staying indoors is natural; no urge to wander like yesterday.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a sandwich and coffee at a nearby café, stay dry indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain is steady until 2pm; Mei seeks shelter and a quick meal. Low energy from yesterday means no detour for a big social lunch—just fueling up.

beats.activity: Back to brewing: bottling line, labeling, inventory check.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift continues; rain is clearing by 2pm, so no weather urgency to leave early. Standard closing time for a Tuesday shift.

beats.activity: Commute home via I-90 westbound, SR-520 now reopened.
beats.reasoning: Evening commute back to Redmond; SR-520 closure lifted, but still budgeting 55 min for post-rain traffic and fatigue. Mei is ready to decompress.

beats.activity: Quick home meal: prep dinner, eat at home.
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday carries forward; Mei opts for a simple dinner at home rather than going out. Self-care after a rainy day.

beats.activity: Film photography hobby: organize recent rolls, check camera equipment, plan next shoot.; Video gaming: play Stardew Valley or similar relaxing game.
beats.reasoning: Mei mentioned itching to shoot more film yesterday; a rainy evening is perfect for indoor hobby time. Aligns with her photography passion.

beats.activity: Wind down: tend rooftop garden one more time, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Mei's rooftop garden is a ritual; a final check before sleep is consistent with her character. Rain has cleared by evening, so she can step outside comfortably.

diary:
Woke up to that familiar grey drizzle and checked on my basil and mint—they're loving this wet weather, even if I'm not. The commute was a mess with SR-520 down, but I dodged it by heading west on I-90 instead. Lucky call, honestly.

At Stoup, it was a solid brewing day. Got the fermentation tanks prepped and dialed in the pH readings. There's something meditative about the precision of it all, especially when the weather's this gloomy outside. Grabbed a sandwich at Molly Moon's during lunch and just sat there watching the rain streak the windows.

Bottling line in the afternoon flew by. My hands know that rhythm by now. By the time I headed home, SR-520 was back open and traffic was flowing again. Made a quick stir-fry, then lost myself in organizing my film rolls and tinkering with my camera gear. Played some Stardew Valley too—felt right for a Tuesday like this. Ended the night back on the rooftop, watering things in the dark. Felt peaceful.
thought_process: The SR-520 closure threw me this morning, but I stayed calm—I knew the I-90 route would be faster anyway. Weather like this actually steadies me; there's no pressure to do anything ambitious, just focus on the work. At the brewery, I was methodical about the tanks because quality control matters, and honestly, that focus helped shake off the greyness. Lunch indoors felt like the right call too; no point fighting the rain. By evening, I needed to decompress, so I split my time between the technical side of photography—organizing and planning—and something passive like Stardew. Both scratched different itches. The rooftop garden check before bed grounded me, literally and figuratively.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
